data.description:
Add `cmux snapshot` / `cmux restore` / `cmux list-snapshots` subcommands that capture a workspace's layout + per-surface state to JSON and rebuild it later — with bit-exact Claude session resume via `cc --resume <session-id>`.

**Why:** `claude --resume <id>` rehydrates full context and history, but nobody uses it because nobody tracks the IDs. Pair it with a layout snapshot and "come back to this tomorrow" becomes `cmux restore morning-work`.

**Plan:** docs/c11mux-snapshot-restore-plan.md

**Verified preconditions:**
- `cc --resume <id>` works (cc is an alias; flags pass through).
- SessionStart hook receives `session_id` on stdin JSON.
- `terminal_type = claude-code` is already being written to surface manifests today.

**Key design choices:**
- Agent-agnostic snapshot: reads `cmux tree --all --json` + per-surface manifests. No private files, no title heuristics.
- Claude session ID captured by an **operator-installed** SessionStart hook that writes `claude.session_id` + `cwd` to the surface manifest. c11mux does not install the hook (principle). Hook is documented in the `cmux` skill.
- `cmux tree --json` is flat — each pane carries `split_path` breadcrumbs. Restore reconstructs the binary split tree from the paths.
- Known-type restart registry inside `cmux restore`: `claude-code` + session_id → `cc --resume <id>`. Extensible per-agent without schema changes.
- Prior art: sanghun0724/cmux-claude-skills. Not adopting directly — its implementation uses c11mux's private session JSON, spinner-char detection, and fuzzy session-ID matching. We use the manifest instead.

**Phases:**
1. Subcommands + terminal surfaces + single workspace + Claude resume.
2. Browser/markdown surfaces + `--all` flag.
3. Skill docs + SessionStart hook snippet.
4. codex/kimi/opencode registry rows.

**Principle check:** fits "unopinionated about the terminal" — operates on c11mux primitives, writes only to `~/.cmux-snapshots/`, does not touch tenant settings files.

**Related:** supersedes CMUX-4 (manual Claude session index) via the hook-driven approach. Restore preserves CMUX-11 pane manifests + CMUX-14 lineage chains verbatim.
data.title: c11mux snapshot & restore: bit-exact Claude session resume + layout rebuild

data.from: c11mux snapshot & restore: bit-exact Claude session resume + layout rebuild
data.to: c11mux workspace persistence: Blueprints (declarative) + Snapshots (live state) + session resume

provenance.reason: Fold Blueprint concept (Markdown workspace definitions) into ticket per discussion; note supersession of CMUX-4 and CMUX-5

data.to:
Add `cmux workspace new`, `cmux snapshot`, `cmux restore`, and `cmux list-snapshots` subcommands that support two distinct persistence concepts — **Blueprints** (declarative Markdown definitions of initial workspace shape) and **Snapshots** (exact serialized live state) — unified under a single workspace-persistence story with bit-exact Claude session resume.

**Why:** `claude --resume <id>` rehydrates full context but nobody tracks the IDs. Pair it with declarative Blueprints (for starting fresh work) and auto-captured Snapshots (for "come back to this tomorrow") and the terminal becomes a persistent workspace, not a disposable shell.

**Plan:** `docs/c11mux-snapshot-restore-plan.md` (update to reflect Blueprint addition).

## Two concepts, one system

| | Blueprint | Snapshot |
|---|---|---|
| Purpose | Define initial workspace shape | Preserve exact live state |
| Format | Markdown (Obsidian-compatible) | JSON |
| Author | Human or agent (hand-written or via `cmux workspace export-blueprint`) | Auto-generated by c11mux |
| Storage | Per-repo (`.cmux/blueprints/*.md`) + per-user (`~/.config/cmux/blueprints/*.md`) | Per-user (`~/.cmux-snapshots/`) |
| Lifecycle | Checked into git, shareable, version-controlled | Opaque, personal, crash-recovery oriented |
| Applied | At workspace creation only | On restart / manual restore |
| Idempotency | Creates the workspace as defined; no merge-into-live semantics | Rehydrates exact tree from JSON |

Both reuse the **known-type restart registry** (`claude-code` + session_id → `cc --resume <id>`) so a Blueprint can embed a `claude.session_id` field and resume the same context a Snapshot would.

## Blueprint format (sketch)

Markdown with YAML frontmatter and structured sections. Exact schema TBD during Phase 2. Example shape:

```markdown
---
name: debug-auth
description: Auth module debugging layout
---

## Panes
- main  | `cc`              | cwd: ~/repo          | claude.session_id: abc123
- logs  | `tail -f log.txt` | split: right of main
- tests | `vitest --watch`  | split: below logs
```

**Idempotency rule:** Blueprints apply only at workspace creation. Once alive, the workspace is the user's to mutate. "Re-apply" = close and reopen.

## New-workspace picker

Empty state of `cmux workspace new` (with no args) shows a merged, recency-sorted picker:

1. Per-repo Blueprints (in-tree, shareable)
2. Per-user Blueprints (personal muscle memory)
3. Built-in starter Blueprints (ship with c11mux)

Selecting one materializes the workspace. This subsumes the recovery-banner UX from (superseded) CMUX-5.

## Verified preconditions

- `cc --resume <id>` works (cc is an alias; flags pass through).
- SessionStart hook receives `session_id` on stdin JSON.
- `terminal_type = claude-code` is already written to surface manifests today.

## Key design choices

- **Agent-agnostic.** Snapshots read `cmux tree --all --json` + per-surface manifests. No private files, no title heuristics.
- **Observe-from-outside session capture.** Claude session ID written by an **operator-installed** SessionStart hook. c11mux never installs the hook (principle). Hook snippet documented in the `cmux` skill.
- **Flat tree with breadcrumbs.** `cmux tree --json` is flat; each pane carries `split_path`. Restore reconstructs the binary split tree from paths.
- **Known-type restart registry.** `claude-code` + session_id → `cc --resume <id>`. Extensible per agent type without schema changes; Blueprints and Snapshots share this registry.
- **Markdown for Blueprints, JSON for Snapshots.** Blueprints are authored/reviewed by humans and agents; Snapshots are machine output. Different audiences, different formats.
- **No merge-into-live semantics.** Blueprints only apply at creation; Snapshots only apply at restore. No ambiguous diff-and-sync behavior.

## Phases

1. **Core subcommands + Snapshots.** `cmux snapshot`, `cmux restore`, `cmux list-snapshots`. Terminal surfaces, single workspace, Claude resume via restart registry.
2. **Blueprint format + picker.** Define Blueprint Markdown schema and parser. `cmux workspace new --blueprint <path>`. New-workspace picker shows recent Blueprints. `cmux workspace export-blueprint` captures live layout as a Blueprint for an agent or human to commit.
3. **Browser/markdown surfaces + `--all` flag.** Extend both Blueprints and Snapshots to non-terminal surfaces.
4. **Skill docs + SessionStart hook snippet.** Document Blueprint authoring conventions in the `cmux` skill. Document the operator-installed hook.
5. **codex/kimi/opencode registry rows.** Add per-agent restart behavior.

## Out of scope (deferred to own tickets)

- **c11mux-manager agent personality** — a dedicated agent whose job is authoring and evolving Blueprints, plus a skill file other agents use to read/write them. Separate ticket once Blueprint format stabilizes.
- **Lattice↔Blueprint frontmatter seam** — ticket frontmatter field (`workspace:`) that auto-materializes the Blueprint on "start work." Minimal version (Markdown link in the ticket body → operator clicks to open) works without any integration. Frontmatter seam deferred until we feel the pain of not having it.

## Principle check

- "Unopinionated about the terminal": operates on c11mux primitives, writes only to `~/.cmux-snapshots/` (Snapshots) and `.cmux/blueprints/` or `~/.config/cmux/blueprints/` (Blueprints). Does not touch tenant tool settings.
- Observe-from-outside: Claude session capture via operator-installed hook, never c11mux-installed.

## Supersedes

- **CMUX-4** (manual Claude session index) — hook-driven capture in this ticket replaces JSONL discovery/disambiguation logic.
- **CMUX-5** (recovery UI banner) — subsumed by the new-workspace picker + restart registry.

Restore preserves CMUX-11 pane manifests + CMUX-14 lineage chains verbatim.

## Prior art

sanghun0724/cmux-claude-skills — not adopting directly; its implementation uses c11mux's private session JSON, spinner-char detection, and fuzzy session-ID matching. We use the public manifest instead.

drolosoft/cmux-resurrect (crex) — community tool for upstream manaflow-ai/cmux with an adjacent design (save/restore + Markdown "Blueprints" + watch daemon + template gallery). Inspired the Blueprint/Snapshot split named here. We deliberately keep the c11mux primitive narrower: snapshot/restore + Blueprint format as core primitives; templates, REPL, and daemon remain ecosystem territory.
